metadata.description: Receptor Rounding Blends — purpose-built mini-blends that activate receptor pathways universally missing from cannabis terpene profiles. Usable standalone for targeted aromatherapy OR as Step 1 in the pre-loading protocol before a strain-matched blend.
metadata.designPrinciple: Strain blends stay 100% pure. Rounding blends are a separate layer — like a pharmacological warm-up. Minimum drops, maximum receptor coverage.
metadata.standardFormat: 5-drop diffuser charge OR diluted in carrier oil for topical/inhaler
metadata.dataSource: Receptor convergence analysis of 23 Solful strains against 15 neuroreceptor targets

targetReceptors.significance: Preserves acetylcholine → memory and attention. Counters THC-induced short-term memory impairment (Russo 2011 entourage hypothesis).
Neuroprotective signaling. Restores CREB phosphorylation in hippocampus.

pharmacology.convergenceNote: Rosemary delivers DUAL AChE inhibition (eucalyptol + pinene). This is the only rounding blend with two terpenes targeting the same enzyme — true pharmacological stacking.
keyStudies: Moss & Oliver 2012 — rosemary aroma improved cognitive performance; plasma 1,8-cineole >75 ng/mL correlated with objective improvement
Li Q et al. — forest bathing (alpha-pinene inhalation) increased NK cell activity for 30 days
Perry et al. 2000 — alpha-pinene AChE inhibition IC₅₀ = 0.44 mM

oils.name: Rosemary ct. cineole
oils.note: Choose the cineole chemotype specifically — it has 35-55% eucalyptol vs. other chemotypes

oils.name: Eucalyptus
oils.note: Reinforces eucalyptol delivery for AChE saturation

aromaProfile.character: Clean and invigorating — like walking through a Mediterranean herb garden
aromaProfile.clashRisk: Very low. Herbal freshness complements virtually all strain blends.
aromaProfile.pairsBestWith: Citrus-forward strains (Carambola, Satsuma Sherbet) and pine strains (Avenue of the Giants, Black Lime Chem)

preLoadingProtocol.timing: Start diffusing 10 min before strain blend; overlap is fine

strainPairings.why: Limonene-forward + Clarity Round adds missing AChE for citrus-cerebral clarity
CBD strain already has focus intent; Clarity adds cognitive receptor coverage
Has pinene but Clarity Round adds eucalyptol for dual AChE stacking
Alert/joyful intent benefits from neuroprotective layer

safetyNotes: Rosemary: avoid during pregnancy and with epilepsy
Eucalyptus: not for use around children under 6
Both oils are food-grade at these concentrations

Calm Round.intent: Cold-receptor analgesia, pain desensitization, physical comfort

targetReceptors.significance: Cold receptor activation → analgesic cooling sensation. Cross-desensitizes TRPV1 (heat/pain receptor), reducing pain sensitivity.
Irritant/pain receptor. Initial activation followed by desensitization → net analgesic effect. TRPA1 antagonism associated with anxiolytic effects.
Anti-inflammatory enzyme inhibition (same target as ibuprofen). Eugenol provides this as a bonus pathway.

pharmacology.convergenceNote: Menthol's TRPM8 activation cross-desensitizes TRPV1, which means if the strain already has myrcene (a TRPV1 modulator), the Calm Round creates a complementary pain-modulation stack: menthol cools → TRPV1 desensitizes → myrcene modulates the desensitized receptor.
keyStudies: Alammar et al. 2019 — peppermint oil meta-analysis for IBS symptom reduction
Eccles et al. 2003 — menthol nasal decongestion via cold receptor activation
Eugenol — FDA-approved dental analgesic; gold standard for tooth pain

oils.name: Peppermint
oils.note: Use sparingly — menthol is potent and can dominate

oils.name: Clove
oils.note: Single drop is sufficient; clove is intense. The secondary BCP reinforces any strain's caryophyllene.

aromaProfile.character: The sensation of stepping from a warm spice kitchen into crisp mountain air
aromaProfile.clashRisk: Moderate. Mint and clove are assertive. Best used in a separate diffuser or inhaler, not mixed into the strain blend.
aromaProfile.pairsBestWith: Heavy/sedative strains (Pinnacle, Black Lime Chem, Mule Fuel) where body comfort is the goal

standalone.duration: 15-30 minutes diffusion, or topical in carrier oil
standalone.timing: As needed; evening preferred for pain/recovery

preLoadingProtocol.timing: Start 5-10 min before strain blend; menthol onset is rapid

strainPairings.why: Deepest sedation strain; Calm Round adds pain pathway for full body surrender
Highest myrcene (2.22%); menthol cross-desensitizes TRPV1 that myrcene modulates
Heavy sleepy strain; pain rounding completes the body experience
Quiet peace intent; Calm Round adds physical comfort layer

safetyNotes: Peppermint: can be overwhelming — never exceed 2 drops in a 5-drop blend
Clove: can cause irritation at high doses — 1 drop max
Both oils are food-grade at these concentrations
Avoid Calm Round if using topical capsaicin products (TRPV1 interaction)

Ease Round.intent: GABA-A convergence for anxiolysis, sedation, and emotional softening

targetReceptors.significance: The primary inhibitory neurotransmitter receptor. Linalool binds the benzodiazepine site (allosteric), enhancing GABA's own effect without replacing it. When combined with myrcene (direct GABA-A modulator, present in 91% of strains), this creates SUPRA-ADDITIVE anxiolysis.
targetReceptors.strainGap: Only 2/23 strains missing GABA-A entirely, BUT 14/23 have Myrcene without Linalool — convergence opportunity

pharmacology.convergenceNote: This is the strongest pharmacological case for cross-terpene pre-loading. Allosteric + direct modulation on the same receptor = 1.3x supra-additive bonus. Linalool primes the benzodiazepine site; when myrcene arrives (via strain blend), GABA transmission is amplified beyond what either terpene achieves alone.
keyStudies: Harada et al. 2018 — BREAKTHROUGH: linalool anxiolytic effects are EXCLUSIVELY olfactory. Anosmic mice show zero response. Non-olfactory routes fail.
Flumazenil completely blocks linalool anxiolytic effect — confirms benzodiazepine site mechanism
R-linalool (lavender) has 9x lower odor threshold than S-linalool (coriander) — lavender is pharmacologically superior
Silexan (standardized lavender oil) — multiple RCTs showing anxiolytic effects comparable to paroxetine
pharmacology.criticalConstraint: OLFACTORY-ONLY PATHWAY. Linalool MUST be inhaled to reach the olfactory epithelium → piriform cortex → amygdala. Oral, topical, or any non-nasal route is ineffective for GABA-A activation. This is the ONLY rounding blend with a strict delivery requirement.

oils.name: Lavender
oils.note: R-linalool from lavender is 9x more potent than S-linalool from coriander. The linalyl acetate in lavender oil may provide supra-additive anxiolytic effects with linalool (Silexan evidence).

aromaProfile.character: The scent of a lavender field at dusk — immediately recognizable, universally soothing
aromaProfile.clashRisk: Very low. Lavender is the most universally compatible essential oil. It softens and rounds any blend without dominating.
aromaProfile.pairsBestWith: Myrcene-dominant strains (Mule Fuel, Glitter Bomb, Avenue of the Giants) where GABA-A convergence is maximized

standalone.duration: 15-30 minutes diffusion minimum for olfactory saturation
standalone.timing: Evening preferred; avoid within 2 hours of needing peak alertness
standalone.dosing: Aroma at detection threshold is sufficient (~1-2 mg equivalent). More is not necessarily better — olfactory habituation begins at 30+ minutes.

preLoadingProtocol.timing: Start diffusing 15 min before strain blend. The 15-min window allows peak GABA-A occupancy in amygdala/hippocampus before THC arrives.

strainPairings.why: Highest myrcene (2.22%) + Ease Round = maximum GABA-A convergence. Allosteric (linalool) + direct (myrcene) = supra-additive sedation.
High myrcene (1.23%) with body/mind split intent — Ease Round deepens the body ease
Already has bisabolol (GABA-A modulator) — adding linalool creates TRIPLE GABA-A convergence
Very high myrcene (1.94%) — Ease Round buffers the high-energy intent with anxiety safety net
Warm social strain with myrcene; Ease Round prevents social anxiety without dimming euphoria

safetyNotes: Lavender is among the safest essential oils — well tolerated by virtually everyone
MUST be diffused or inhaled, NOT ingested for this purpose
Olfactory habituation begins at ~30 minutes — don't exceed 30 min pre-load
If using Silexan capsules (oral), note this activates a DIFFERENT pathway than olfactory — both can be used but they work through separate mechanisms

twoStepPreLoading.description: The full pre-loading protocol preserves strain blend integrity while maximizing receptor coverage.

steps.name: Receptor Rounding
steps.method: Separate diffuser, personal inhaler, or steam bowl
steps.instruction: Choose 1-2 rounding blends based on session intent. Add drops to diffuser and begin inhaling. This opens receptor pathways that the strain blend doesn't cover.

steps.name: Strain Blend
steps.timing: 5-10 minutes after starting Step 1 (overlap is fine)
steps.method: Personal inhaler, second diffuser, or topical application
steps.instruction: Apply your existing 40-drop strain-matched blend exactly as designed. Do not modify the recipe. The rounding blend and strain blend work on SEPARATE receptor pathways — they don't compete.

steps.name: Cannabis
steps.instruction: By this point, rounding receptors are primed (AChE inhibited, TRPM8 activated, GABA-A upregulated) and strain-matched terpene receptors are occupied. THC/CBD arrive to a pharmacologically prepared system.

standaloneUse.description: Each rounding blend works as an independent aromatherapy product without cannabis or strain blends. The receptor targeting provides real therapeutic benefit on its own.
standaloneUse.method: Add drops to diffuser, personal inhaler, or dilute in carrier oil (jojoba, fractionated coconut) at 2-3% for topical use.
standaloneUse.duration: 15-40 minutes diffusion for olfactory effects; topical effects last 1-2 hours

diffuserBlend.description: Pre-mixed oil for ultrasonic or nebulizing diffusers
diffuserBlend.dilution: Neat (undiluted) — user adds drops to diffuser water

personalInhaler.description: Cotton-wick nasal inhaler tube for portable, on-demand use
personalInhaler.advantage: Discreet, portable, no diffuser needed. Ideal for Ease Round (olfactory requirement).

rollerBottle.description: Diluted in carrier oil for topical pulse-point application
rollerBottle.advantage: Best for Calm Round (topical menthol/eugenol for localized pain relief)

showerSteamer.description: Effervescent tablet that releases blend in shower steam
showerSteamer.format: Bath bomb form factor, shower floor placement
showerSteamer.advantage: Combines olfactory delivery with warm steam. Excellent for morning Clarity Round or evening Ease Round.

bathSoak.description: Epsom salt base infused with rounding blend
bathSoak.advantage: Full immersion delivery — olfactory + topical. Best for Calm Round (pain) + Ease Round (anxiety).

withAllThreeRounds.remainingGaps: Strain-dependent; typically only CB1 (weak modulator only) and strain-specific singles
